user_query:
Read input/company.json and input/recurring_spec.yaml to get the company and account IDs, then set up a monthly recurring journal template for office rent using those parameters (do not auto-submit). Process recurring as of 2026-02-01 to generate the February draft entry, review it to confirm totals balance, and submit it. Next, read input/abatement.txt (temporary rent abatement for February): cancel the February entry, create an amended draft with the reduced amount, and submit the amended entry. Finally, write all deliverables under output/ as follows:

1) output/journal_lifecycle.json — exact schema:
- company_id (string)
- template_name (string)
- frequency (string)
- as_of_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- initial_amount (string with two decimals)
- amended_amount (string with two decimals)
- stages (array of 5 objects) in this exact order and with these minimum fields:
  a) { "stage": "generated_draft", "status": "draft", "total_debit": "5000.00", "total_credit": "5000.00" }
  b) { "stage": "submitted_original", "status": "submitted" }
  c) { "stage": "cancelled_original", "status": "cancelled" }
  d) { "stage": "amended_draft", "status": "draft", "total_debit": "4500.00", "total_credit": "4500.00" }
  e) { "stage": "submitted_amended", "status": "submitted" }
Populate the amounts based on the input files and ensure the debit and credit match at each draft stage.

2) output/summary.csv — a concise table with columns: stage,status,total_debit,total_credit. Include one row for each stage listed above. For the two draft stages, the totals must exactly match the amounts shown in journal_lifecycle.json (5000.00 for the initial draft and 4500.00 for the amended draft). Leave totals blank for non-draft stages if not applicable.

3) output/notes.md — a 200+ word plain-English explanation of what you did and why, covering: how the recurring template generated the February draft, how you verified the double-entry balance, why you confirmed before submit/cancel/amend, how cancelling reverses entries rather than deleting, and how the amendment created a new draft with the reduced amount before submission. Please explicitly use the terms "double-entry", "recurring template", "submit", "cancel", and "amend" at least once.
